sourceRef: tip:How to explain monthly housing allowance in one minute

slides.headline: Quick Guide: Monthly Housing Allowance
slides.body: Learn how to explain it in 60 seconds.

slides.headline: Understand the Basics
slides.body: The monthly housing allowance is designed to cover basic housing needs.
bullets: It’s based on the cost of living in the state where the student resides.
Amount varies by state and the type of housing (e.g., apartment, house).

slides.headline: Check the Form 22-1999
slides.body: Refer to VA Form 22-1999 to find the exact allowance amount for your state.
bullets: Consult your COE for the latest information.

slides.headline: Use Clear Language
slides.body: When explaining to students, use simple terms.
bullets: Explain that the allowance helps cover the rent or mortgage.
Point out that it doesn’t cover utilities, insurance, or other expenses.

slides.headline: Be Prepared for Variations
slides.body: Students might need help with special cases.
bullets: Discuss with SCO for specific situations (e.g., shared housing).
Remind students to verify their eligibility annually.

slides.headline: Stay Updated
bullets: Check the VA website for updates on housing allowance.
Reach out to your SAA for any new policies.

slides.body: Master the housing allowance. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Learn how to quickly and clearly explain the monthly housing allowance to students in one minute. Use this script to address the most-asked question.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 3, 'Use Clear Language,' as it could benefit from a more direct call to action or a stronger visual element to emphasize its importance.
